examples.text: إِنَّ أُحُدًا جَبَلٌ يُحِبُّنَا وَنُحِبُّهُ وَهُوَ عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ الْجَنَّةِ وَعَيْرٌ عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ النَّارِ.
examples.english: ʾuḥud is a mountain which loves us and we love it, and it stands at one of the gates of Paradise. And ʿayr stands at one of the gates of Hell.’”

senses.gloss: mouth of a rivulet, of a watering-trough, of a water-tank or of an other source of water

etymology.text: Borrowed from Aramaic תַּרְעָא (tarʿā, “opening, gate”). Doublet of ثُغْرَة (ṯuḡra).
